2026-04-20T06:53:24.5460502Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T06:53:24.5461029Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T06:53:24.5461441Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T06:53:24.5461798Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T06:53:24.5462095Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T06:53:24.5462326Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T06:53:24.5462490Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T06:53:24.5462676Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-20T06:53:24.5463089Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-20T06:53:24.5463560Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-20T06:53:24.5463812Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. 2026-04-20T06:54:46.8274404Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T06:54:31.606248737Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58357,\"title\":\"Mgmt generator should preserve WritableSubResource ID flattening for backward compatibility\",\"body\":\"## Background\\n\\nDuring the DNS Resolver mgmt TypeSpec migration, the generated model for `VirtualNetworkDnsForwardingRuleset` now exposes:\\n\\n- `VirtualNetworkLink : WritableSubResource`\\n\\nwhile the previously shipped public API exposed:\\n\\n- `VirtualNetworkLinkId : ResourceIdentifier`\\n\\nWe had to add custom compatibility code in `sdk/dnsresolver/Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver/src/Custom/VirtualNetworkDnsForwardingRuleset.cs` to preserve the old property and avoid a breaking change.\\n\\n## Root cause\\n\\nThe current mgmt generator/flattening behavior appears to flatten the first-level wrapper type, but it does not preserve the older ARM-style projection for non-generated subresource models like `WritableSubResource`, whose public shape is effectively just:\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic ResourceIdentifier Id { get; set; }\\n```\\n\\nBecause of that, callers now see `VirtualNetworkLink` instead of the previously exposed `VirtualNetworkLinkId` convenience property, and the migration needs handwritten shim code.\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\n\\nWhen the generator encounters a flattenable non-generated type such as `WritableSubResource`, it should be able to flatten/project that shape in a backward-compatible way as well, so migrations do not require custom shims for `...Id` properties.\\n\\n## Why this matters\\n\\nThis would let us preserve existing ARM public API shape automatically and reduce custom migration-only code.\\n\\n## Example\\n\\n- Package: `Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver`\\n- Type: `VirtualNetworkDnsForwardingRuleset`\\n- Current workaround: custom partial that adds back `VirtualNetworkLinkId =\\u0026gt; VirtualNetworkLink?.Id`\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58357\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"comments\":1,\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-20T06:52:05Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-20T06:54:20Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-20T06:54:46.8291561Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T06:54:31.839377626Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58357,\"title\":\"Mgmt generator should preserve WritableSubResource ID flattening for backward compatibility\",\"body\":\"## Background\\n\\nDuring the DNS Resolver mgmt TypeSpec migration, the generated model for `VirtualNetworkDnsForwardingRuleset` now exposes:\\n\\n- `VirtualNetworkLink : WritableSubResource`\\n\\nwhile the previously shipped public API exposed:\\n\\n- `VirtualNetworkLinkId : ResourceIdentifier`\\n\\nWe had to add custom compatibility code in `sdk/dnsresolver/Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver/src/Custom/VirtualNetworkDnsForwardingRuleset.cs` to preserve the old property and avoid a breaking change.\\n\\n## Root cause\\n\\nThe current mgmt generator/flattening behavior appears to flatten the first-level wrapper type, but it does not preserve the older ARM-style projection for non-generated subresource models like `WritableSubResource`, whose public shape is effectively just:\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic ResourceIdentifier Id { get; set; }\\n```\\n\\nBecause of that, callers now see `VirtualNetworkLink` instead of the previously exposed `VirtualNetworkLinkId` convenience property, and the migration needs handwritten shim code.\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\n\\nWhen the generator encounters a flattenable non-generated type such as `WritableSubResource`, it should be able to flatten/project that shape in a backward-compatible way as well, so migrations do not require custom shims for `...Id` properties.\\n\\n## Why this matters\\n\\nThis would let us preserve existing ARM public API shape automatically and reduce custom migration-only code.\\n\\n## Example\\n\\n- Package: `Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver`\\n- Type: `VirtualNetworkDnsForwardingRuleset`\\n- Current workaround: custom partial that adds back `VirtualNetworkLinkId =\\u0026gt; VirtualNetworkLink?.Id`\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58357\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"comments\":1,\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-20T06:52:05Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-20T06:54:20Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
